title: Europe by Rail — July 2026 (Milan · Lakes · Bernina · Jungfrau · Paris · Provence · Barcelona · Madrid)

legs.note: Book a morning flight so Como is doable the same afternoon.
Arrival-afternoon day trip to Lake Como.

Trenord direct regionale ~2h20 (trenord.it). Jul 6–7 are open northern-lakes buffer (Como/Maggiore/Garda or Milan) before the Bernina — now relaxed, no Italy backtrack. Overnight Tirano for the 08:06 Bernina. Marked manual: Italian domestic regional isn't in the open APIs.
BOOKED — Bernina Express, Tirano 08:06 → Chur 12:31, Jul 9. Panoramic, seat reservation held (rhb.ch).
Onward to the Jungfrau region right off the Bernina (~3h45 via Zürich/Bern + Interlaken Ost). Camp in Lauterbrunnen valley.
Jungfrau → Paris via Interlaken Ost → Bern → TGV Lyria. ~6.5–7h. Two camping nights (Jul 9, 10) first.
South of France (replaces Amalfi). Direct TGV ~3h15. Base for Provence — Cassis/Calanques, Aix, Avignon day trips. (Swap to Avignon if you prefer villages over a city base.)
Direct TGV ~4h40 (~1–2/day) — the reason Provence beats Nice here. Book early.
AVE ~2.5–3h, frequent. Renfe has no public API — book on renfe.com / Trainline.
Fly home. Trip now ends Jul 19 (South-of-France stop added 2 days vs the Amalfi version).
